preamble: The vocalization of a low vowel to a labiovelar glide often occurs in diphthongization environments, specifically in historical developments where open vowels become rounded or retracted.

examples.note: Low vowels evolve into labiovelar glides in specific diphthongization environments.

sources: Ladefoged, P., & Maddieson, I. (1996). The Sounds of the World's Languages.
